---
title: "Week 2 — LLM literacy for bio"
subtitle: "How the tools work, and how to prompt them"
---

## Topics

- How LLMs work, at a working level.
- Prompting patterns that actually help.
- Tool use and agents — a short tour.
- Ethics and limits in a research context.

## Before class

Read:

- [How LLMs work](../literacy/how-llms-work.qmd)
- [Prompting](../literacy/prompting.qmd)
- [Tool use & agents](../literacy/tool-use-and-agents.qmd)
- [Ethics & limits](../literacy/ethics-and-limits.qmd)

## In class

- Short lecture: what's under the hood.
- Live prompt clinic: attendees bring a prompt that disappointed them; we diagnose.
- Demo: a minimal tool-using agent (e.g., bibliography cleanup).

## After class

- **Deliverable**: a prompt-engineering exercise. Take a recurring task from your work. Produce (a) a weak prompt, (b) a strong prompt using patterns from [Prompting](../literacy/prompting.qmd), (c) outputs from each, (d) a short critique.

## Supplementary

*TODO.*
